headline: Notre Dame's Ground Game Overwhelms Arkansas in Convincing Week 2 Win
body: Notre Dame's offensive identity crystallized Saturday in a 35-10 victory over Arkansas, with Hollywood Smothers muscling through the Razorback defense for two touchdown runs and establishing a physical tone that Arkansas never answered. The Irish rushed for 145 yards on the afternoon, not gaudy by any standard, but deployed with surgical precision to set up manageable third downs and keep the chains moving when the passing game faced coverage adjustments.

Jackson's night for Arkansas told the story of a quarterback fighting an uphill battle. He completed 13 of 30 attempts for 249 yards but managed no touchdowns, and more troubling, his receivers—including Clay's respectable 91-yard haul—operated in isolation rather than as a coordinated attack. The Razorbacks accumulated only 86 rushing yards across the game, forcing Jackson into long-yardage situations and eliminating the play-action looks that could have kept Carr honest.

Notre Dame's Carr was efficient without spectacular, completing 24 of 39 for 357 yards and two touchdowns, the kind of workmanlike performance that reflects offensive patience and balance. Fuller's 92 receiving yards and one touchdown gave the Irish a second receiving threat, but Smothers remained the engine. Arkansas had no answer for his downhill running on early downs, and once the Irish established that tone, the game's direction became inevitable.
turningPoint: Smothers' first touchdown run, coming early in the second quarter with Notre Dame leading only 7-3, shifted momentum decisively by validating the Irish's power-running scheme and forcing Arkansas into pass-first desperation.

unsungHero.name: Clay
unsungHero.why: He led Arkansas receivers with 91 yards on only four catches, showing route precision and separation that went wasted in an architecture that couldn't protect Jackson long enough to develop timing.
